headline: Six months on the UAE board.
standfirst: Pulled from Semrush's Domain Overview for the AE database on June 3, 2026. Authority Score is holding at 25. Organic keywords are growing (+3.6% week-over-week). Organic traffic dipped from a February peak of ~510 to today's 428 — a pattern the brand-heavy traffic mix made visible early and the foundation work deployed June 1 is meant to reverse.

feature_chart.title: Organic traffic, six months · UAE database
feature_chart.subtitle: Estimated monthly organic visits to cmsprime.com from .ae searchers
feature_chart.caption: Organic traffic peaked at ~510 in mid-February then stepped down to a ~430-450 band through April-May, settling at 428 in early June. The branded line (96.7% of total organic) tracks underneath at ~414 today — confirming the mix is brand-heavy. The hreflang and Organization schema work deployed June 1 should start lifting non-brand queries on the July 1 reading.

prose.title: The number that matters, and the one we're watching.
paragraphs: Authority Score is holding at 25. Semrush rebuilds the score on a roughly 90-day rolling window, and the disavow + 301 recovery work we landed June 3 won't show up here until late August. Expect this number flat through July.

Organic keyword count is the leading indicator and it's moving in the right direction — 28 to 29 this week, +3.6%. New keywords entering the index means Google is processing the FAQ schema, hreflang, and Organization enrichment that went live June 1. Position movement on those keywords follows on a 2-4 week lag.

Organic traffic is the lagging indicator. 428 visits this week, down 1.8% from last week and down meaningfully from the February peak of ~510. This is the metric to watch through July. If keywords keep climbing but traffic stays flat, it means Google indexed the new structure but the rankings haven't lifted enough to drive volume. If traffic moves up alongside keywords, the foundation work is being rewarded.

pull_quote.text: Keywords are the leading indicator. Traffic follows, on a 2-4 week lag.
